economic_notes: Alexander First Nation Business Trust reorganized as of January 1, 2024 forming four new partnerships; oil and gas royalties flow through Capital and Revenue Trust Funds; GBEs include gas bar, HDD, contracting, asphalt/concrete, commercial developments, building solutions, investment holdings, and campground. Replacement reserve underfunded by $1,391,272.
notes: Audit is qualified due to inability to obtain sufficient evidence on GBE investments and equity income. Prior period restatement for tangible capital assets, deferred revenue, and GBE investments. Comparative figures restated. Three consolidated subsidiaries (Alexander Employment and Labour Services Ltd., 824750 Alberta Ltd. o/a Alexander Forestry Services, Alexander First Nation Farm Enterprises Ltd.) and 8 GBEs under Alexander First Nation Business Trust. CMHC deferred revenue of $7,912,284 is significant. Trust account claim settlement of $7,470,269 from prior years mostly spent, $1,000,000 remaining in GIC for Youth Centre. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($35,326,864) + Health Canada/ISC Health ($8,759,126) + Province of Alberta ($3,832,243) + CMHC ($9,521,181) + Service Canada ($408,725) + 1905 LSC Trust ($2,050,000) + Alexander Community External Relations ($1,647,376) - inter-entity allocation to ABC Group ($1,200,000) + other adjustments; OSR includes forestry, farm, property tax, other revenue, and trust withdrawals net of government transfers. Schedule of remuneration not included in provided text.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
